source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/05_해외 등록 서류 (영업팀 F-up)/260520 우크라이나(Licarno) 23G, P1 - 이창민 팀장님/260527 1차 제출본/3.2 [Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector] Technical Document/A-TCF-5-156 Test Reports/1. [23G series] IEC 60601-1 CB Certi & Test report (ed. 3.2).pdf

document.title: IECEE CB Test Certificate NO129490 and IEC 60601-1/IEC 60601-1-6 CB Test Reports REP028549/REP028549_1 for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
document.documentType: CB Test Certificate and CB Scheme safety/usability test reports with component CB certificate attachments
document.revisionOrVersion: Certificate No. NO129490; Test Report No. REP028549; IEC 60601-1-6 Report No. REP028549_1; TRF No. IEC60601_1U; replaces REP001824; User manual A-TCF-5-130 Version 0.1; component attachments include NO82590/A1/M2, NO96755/M1, E302267-D1020-1/A1/C1-CB, DK-70440-UL, and DK-70440-M1-UL

sourceSummary.oneLine: IECEE CB certificate NO129490 and Nemko Korea reports REP028549/REP028549_1 document IEC 60601-1 basic safety, essential performance, software, usability, national deviations, and component evidence for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory submission evidence for CB Scheme certification, IEC 60601-1 and IEC 60601-1-6 conformity, model coverage, risk management, usability engineering, IEC 62304 software lifecycle, electrical/mechanical safety, IP protection, national deviations, user manual claims, and component certifications for power supply, AC/DC adapter, and battery pack.
keyPoints: CB Certificate NO129490 and Test Report REP028549 were issued on 2024-02-20 for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
The product was found in conformity with IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD1:2012 + AMD2:2020 and IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + AMD1:2013 + AMD2:2020, and the report states EN 60601-1 and EN 60601-1-6 fulfillment.
A1717MCW is the representative main model; A1417MCW and F1417MCW are variants with listed differences in dimensions, weight, pixel data, enclosure material, main board, and gate board.

The equipment is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of adult and pediatric human anatomy and is not for mammography; the manual additionally contraindicates direct cardiac application, infants, and pregnant women.

The detector is classified as internally powered, Type B applied part, IP54 in the test report, portable, continuous operation, and not intended for oxygen-rich environments, flammable anesthetics, flammable agents, or operator sterilization.
Risk management was documented against ISO 14971:2019 and EN ISO 14971:2019/A11:2021 and reviewed by Nemko; expected service life in the risk management file is 5 years.

Software was declared and reviewed as IEC 62304 Class B, with PEMS lifecycle, validation, configuration management, problem resolution, IT-network, cybersecurity-related, and risk-management controls assessed as passing.
IEC 60601-1-6 report REP028549_1 is process/file-inspection evidence for usability engineering and states no physical testing because it is a process standard.
National differences were applied for United States, Canada, Japan, and Korea; CENELEC/EN requirements are also addressed.
Component attachments provide CB evidence for DEPS9601/DEPS9601A power supplies, BM090S15F/BridgePower AC/DC adapters, and ELT-3S1PA rechargeable Li-ion battery pack.
EMC and biocompatibility are manufacturer-declared or not evaluated in the IEC 60601-1 report chunks and should not be treated as independently tested by this report.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Applicant, manufacturer, and factory are listed as H&abyz Co., Ltd.; IEC 60601-1-6 report lists applicant as H&abyz Co., Ltd. and manufacturer as same as applicant.

entities.name: Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Report REP028549 and REP028549_1 list Nemko Korea Co., Ltd. as the testing laboratory preparing the report and CB Testing Laboratory.

entities.name: Nemko
entities.evidence: The CB Test Certificate is issued by the National Certification Body and the report states it is issued under the responsibility of www.nemko.com.

entities.name: Sweet Yuan
entities.evidence: Certificate NO129490 is dated 20-02-2024 with signature Sweet Yuan, Certification department.

entities.name: Sang Choon Kim
entities.evidence: Page 2 identifies Sang Choon, Kim as tested by, Project Handler.

entities.name: Hyun Woo Park
entities.evidence: Page 2 identifies Hyun Woo, Park as approved by, Verificator.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: The certificate product and test item description are Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.

entities.name: A1717MCW
entities.evidence: The main model is A1717MCW; all testing was conducted with main model A1717MCW as representative unless otherwise specified.

entities.name: A1417MCW
entities.evidence: Model/Type Ref includes A1417MCW; variants A1417MCW and F1417MCW are identical except listed differences.

entities.name: F1417MCW
entities.evidence: Model/Type Ref includes F1417MCW; variants A1417MCW and F1417MCW are identical except listed differences.

entities.name: DEPS9601
entities.evidence: CB certificate NO82590/A1/M2 identifies product as Power supply, model/type reference DEPS9601, with input 1.0 A, 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz, Class I, output 3 A, 16 Vdc.

entities.name: DEPS9601A
entities.evidence: CB certificate NO96755/M1 identifies product as Power supply, model/type reference DEPS9601A, with AC or 24 Vdc input and 3 A, 16 Vdc output.

entities.name: BM090S15F
entities.evidence: Ratings and attachments identify AC/DC adapter model BM090S15F; Attachment 6 heading states CBTC-TR and attachments for AC/DC adapter, model BM090S15F.

entities.name: ELT-3S1PA
entities.evidence: Attachment 7 identifies rechargeable Li-ion battery pack model/type reference ELT-3S1PA / 3ICP5/43/97, rated 11.55 Vdc, 3200 mAh, 36.96 Wh.

entities.name: ELENTEC CO LTD
entities.evidence: Battery CB certificate lists ELENTEC CO LTD as applicant, manufacturer, and factory.

entities.name: SAMSUNG SDI CO., LTD.
entities.evidence: IEC 62133 critical components table lists lithium-ion rechargeable battery cell PGF494397H++ manufactured by SAMSUNG SDI CO., LTD.

entities.name: DAEAN ELECOM CO., LTD.
entities.type: Power supply applicant, manufacturer, and factory
entities.evidence: CB certificates and reports identify DAEAN ELECOM CO., LTD. as applicant, manufacturer, and factory for power supply models DEPS9601 and DEPS9601A.

entities.name: BRIDGEPOWER CORP
entities.type: AC/DC adapter applicant, manufacturer, and factory
entities.evidence: Attachment 6 CB certificates list BRIDGEPOWER CORP as applicant, manufacturer, and factory for the AC/DC adaptor.

entities.name: UL International Demko A/S
entities.evidence: Attachment 6 test report lists CB Testing Laboratory: UL International Demko A/S, Borupvang 5A, 2750 Ballerup, Denmark.

entities.name: UL (Demko)
entities.evidence: Battery CB Test Certificate DK-70440-M1-UL is issued under IECEE CB Scheme by UL Demko, Borupvang 5A DK-2750 Ballerup, Denmark.

entities.name: JaviTech e.K
entities.evidence: Service Center section lists European Authorized Representative (EC REP) – JaviTech e.K, Sachsenhausener Straße 16, 65824 Schwalbach am Taunus, Germany.

entities.name: Emergo Global Representation LLC
entities.evidence: Service Center section lists United States Agent Information – Michael van der woude Emergo Global Representation LLC, Austin, Texas, with USAgent@UL.com.

concepts.name: IECEE CB Scheme conformity
concepts.evidence: A sample was tested and found in conformity with IEC 60601-1 and IEC 60601-1-6, with test report references REP028549 and REP028549_1.

concepts.name: Basic safety and essential performance
concepts.evidence: The test report is for IEC 60601-1 Medical Electrical Equipment Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance.

concepts.name: Representative model testing
concepts.evidence: A1717MCW is identified as the main model; A1417MCW and F1417MCW are variants, and testing was conducted with A1717MCW unless otherwise specified.

concepts.name: Risk management process
concepts.evidence: Risk management results are documented by the manufacturer on the basis of ISO 14971:2019 and EN ISO 14971:2019/A11:2021, reviewed by Nemko, and mapped to A-TCF-5-103, A-TCF-5-104, and A-TCF-5-105.

concepts.name: IEC 62304 software Class B
concepts.evidence: The manufacturer declared software Class B to IEC 62304; clause 4.3 states the software system is classified as software class B.

concepts.name: PEMS development lifecycle
concepts.evidence: PEMS development lifecycle, verification milestones, validation plan, problem resolution, and IT-network connection instructions are documented and marked P.

concepts.name: Usability engineering process
concepts.evidence: Inspection of the usability engineering file verified compliance with IEC 62366-1:2015+A1:2020; IEC 60601-1-6 report states process standard only, no testing.

concepts.name: Type B applied part
concepts.pageTarget: Page 4 and User manual pages 29 and 35
concepts.evidence: The front panel of the detector is classified as Type B applied part; manual states patient contacts the front of the detector in less than 10 minutes.

concepts.name: Ingress protection
concepts.pageTarget: Page 4, Page 75, Page 167, and User manual page 16
concepts.evidence: The IEC 60601-1 report classifies the detector as IP54 and subjects it to IEC 60529 testing; the user manual states the models are rated IP56 according to IEC 60529 when battery cover and wireless conditions are met.

concepts.name: National differences
concepts.evidence: National differences for United States, Canada, and Japan were applied in the report; Attachment 2 lists United States, Canada, Japan, and Korea.

concepts.name: End-use ME system consideration
concepts.evidence: The report requires additional consideration when used with X-ray equipment/system and operating PC in end-use application, and states this test report does not cover ME systems requirements.

concepts.name: Biocompatibility declaration only
concepts.evidence: The manufacturer declared ISO 10993-1 compliance, but biocompatibility is not evaluated as part of this test report and is marked N/E.

concepts.name: EMC declaration and manual guidance
concepts.evidence: The manufacturer declared IEC 60601-1-2 compliance; manual states compliance with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards and provides CISPR 11, immunity, and separation-distance guidance.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Essential performance functions include intended image acquisition, no adjacent two-line or blank image, normal operation when required, and normal manufacturer program operation.

concepts.name: Leakage current compliance
concepts.evidence: Earth leakage, touch current, and patient leakage current measurements were recorded under normal and single fault conditions with Pass remarks.

concepts.name: Dielectric strength
concepts.evidence: MOOP/MOPP dielectric strength table lists insulation areas A through N with no dielectric breakdown after 1 minute.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity wireless access controls
concepts.evidence: Manual says transmitted data is protected using WPA/WPAPSK/WPA2/WPA2PSK and random access is restricted by ID/PW for wireless network connection.

concepts.name: Wired and wireless interfaces
concepts.evidence: Interface section lists wired IEEE 802.3 10/100/1000 BASE-T and wireless IEEE 802.11 a/b/g/n/ac Wi-Fi with 2.4 GHz/5 GHz dual frequency.

concepts.name: Power supply component certification
concepts.evidence: Certificates identify IECEE CB Scheme CB Test Certificates for power supplies DEPS9601 and DEPS9601A, both found in conformity with IEC 60601-1:2005 and AMD1:2012.

concepts.name: Battery IEC 62133 certification
concepts.evidence: CB Test Certificate DK-70440-UL and DK-70440-M1-UL identify rechargeable Li-ion battery pack ELT-3S1PA / 3ICP5/43/97 and state conformity with IEC 62133:2012.

evidence.claim: CB Certificate NO129490 was issued for a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector on 2024-02-20.
evidence.evidenceText: Ref. Certif. No NO129490; Product Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector; Date: 20-02-2024.

evidence.claim: The CB certificate covers models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Model/Type Ref A1717MCW; A1417MCW; F1417MCW.

evidence.claim: The product was found in conformity with IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD1:2012 + AMD2:2020 and IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + AMD1:2013 + AMD2:2020.
evidence.evidenceText: A sample of the product was tested and found to be in conformity with IEC 60601-1-6:2010/AMD1:2013/AMD2:2020 and IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD1:2012/AMD2:2020.

evidence.claim: Test report REP028549 is an IEC 60601-1 CB Scheme report issued by Nemko Korea Co., Ltd. on 2024-02-20.
evidence.evidenceText: Report Number REP028549; Date of issue 2024-02-20; Name of Testing Laboratory preparing the Report: Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.; Test procedure: CB Scheme.

evidence.claim: The report has 192 pages and uses TRF No. IEC60601_1U.
evidence.evidenceText: Total number of pages: 192; Page 2 of 192 Report No. REP028549 TRF No. IEC60601_1U.
evidence.pageTarget: Test report cover page and Page 2

evidence.claim: The report replaces previous test report REP001824 due to updated amendment coverage and address changes.
evidence.evidenceText: This test report No. REP028549 replaces previous original test report No. REP001824 due to updating standard version to add AMD2:2020 and changing applicant, manufacturer and factory address.

evidence.claim: The equipment is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: The equipment is indicated for digital imaging solution for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children; It is not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The user manual contraindicates use for mammography, direct cardiac application, infants, and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Contraindications state not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application, and not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The device consists of the power supply box, digital flat panel X-ray detector, switch box, and exposure switch.
evidence.evidenceText: The equipment consists of power supply box, digital flat panel X-ray detector, switch box and exposure switch.

evidence.claim: The detector and power supply box are intended for the patient environment, while switch box and exposure switch are outside the patient environment.
evidence.evidenceText: The power supply box and digital flat panel X-ray detector are intended for use with patient environment. The switch box and exposure switch are intended for use outside patient environment.

evidence.claim: The device supports two power configurations: DEPS9601/DEPS9601A power supply box with data cable, or BM090S15F AC/DC adapter with ethernet cable.
evidence.evidenceText: Power configuration 1: Power supply box, data cable, detector 3 A, 16 Vdc; Power configuration 2: AC/DC adapter, ethernet cable, detector 6 A, 15 Vdc.

evidence.claim: The detector is classified as internally powered, Type B applied part, IP54, portable, and continuous operation.
evidence.evidenceText: Classifications include internally powered for detector, Type B applied part for front panel of detector, IP54 for detector, and mode of operation continuous operation.

evidence.claim: The power supply box and AC/DC adapter are Class I ME equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Type of protection against electric shock: Class I for power supply box and AC/DC adapter; Page 23 states both are classified as class I ME equipment.

evidence.claim: National differences for the United States, Canada, and Japan were applied in the test report.
evidence.evidenceText: National differences for the following countries have been applied in this test report: United States (US), Canada (CA) and Japan (JP).

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 national deviations were included for United States, Canada, Japan, and Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 2 National deviations lists United States (US), Canada (CA), Japan (JP), and Korea (KR).
The product fulfils the requirements of IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD1:2012 + AMD2:2020 and EN 60601-1:2006 + A1:2013 + A2:2021.

evidence.claim: The main model is A1717MCW and testing generally used A1717MCW as representative for A1417MCW and F1417MCW variants.
evidence.evidenceText: The main model is A1717MCW; variants A1417MCW and F1417MCW are identical except listed differences; all testing was conducted with main model A1717MCW as representative unless otherwise specified.

evidence.claim: Model differences include dimensions, weight, pixel counts, pixel areas, fire enclosure material, main board, and gate board.
evidence.evidenceText: Variants differ by model name, dimension, weight, total pixel number/area, effective pixel number/area, material of fire enclosure, main board and gate board.

evidence.claim: Technical detector specifications include a-Si TFT sensor, CsI scintillator, 140 micrometer pixel pitch, maximum 3.57 lp/mm limiting resolution, and 40-150 kV energy range.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical specification: Sensor type a-Si TFT; Scintillator Csl; Pixel pitch 140 μm; Limiting resolution Max. 3.57 lp/mm; Energy range 40-150 kV.

evidence.claim: The detector sensor technology is indirect a-Si TFT with CsI scintillator, 16-bit A/D conversion, 140 um pixel pitch, and maximum limiting resolution 3.57 lp/mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Electrical Features list sensor type a-Si TFT, scintillator CsI, pixel pitch 140um, limiting resolution max 3.57lp/mm, and A/D conversion 16 bits.

evidence.claim: Operating environmental conditions are 10-35 °C, 20-75% RH, 700-1060 hPa, altitude max 3000 m.
evidence.evidenceText: Operation condition Temperature (10-35) °C, Humidity (20-75)% R.H., Atmospheric pressure (700-1060) hPa, Altitude Max. 3000 m.

evidence.claim: Storage and transportation conditions are -15 to 55 °C, 10-95% RH, and 500-1060 hPa.
evidence.evidenceText: Storage and transportation condition Temperature (-15-55) °C, Humidity (10-95)% R.H., Atmospheric pressure (500-1060) hPa.

evidence.claim: Risk management was based on ISO 14971:2019 and EN ISO 14971:2019/A11:2021 and reviewed by Nemko.
evidence.evidenceText: RM Results are documented by the manufacturer on the basis of ISO 14971:2019 and EN ISO 14971:2019/A11:2021 and are reviewed by Nemko.

evidence.claim: Risk management outputs were mapped to ISO 14971 clauses and passed.
evidence.evidenceText: RM results table maps ISO 14971 clauses 4.1 through 9 to A-TCF-5-103, A-TCF-5-104, and A-TCF-5-105; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Expected service life considered in the risk management file is 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Expected service life stated in risk management file: 5 years was considered.

evidence.claim: The detector system has a five-year lifetime and three-year warranty from sales date.
evidence.evidenceText: Warranty table: Product Name Digital Flat-Panel X-ray Detector; Model Name A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW; Life Time 5 years from sales date; Warranty 3 years from sales date.

evidence.claim: Software forming part of PEMS is identified and classified as IEC 62304 Class B.
evidence.evidenceText: Software forming part of a PEMS identified with a unique identifier; software is classified as Class B to IEC 62304; Software Class: B.

evidence.claim: The medical device software is classified as IEC 62304 software safety class B.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.3 states the software system is classified as software class B; class A and class C lines are N/A and the class B criterion has verdict P.

evidence.claim: No legacy software was considered for this software compliance assessment.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.4 Legacy Software remarks state: No legacy software considered, with N/A verdicts for legacy software activities.

evidence.claim: Software requirements included functional, interface, alarm/message, security, IT-network, maintenance, and regulatory requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 5.2.2 lists software requirements including functional requirements, inputs/outputs, interfaces, software-driven alarms, SECURITY requirements, IT-network aspects, user maintenance, and regulatory requirements, all with verdict P.

evidence.claim: Software release controls included completed verification, residual anomalies, released software version, release environment, archived software/configuration items, and delivery protection against corruption or unauthorized change.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 5.8.1 through 5.8.8 reference Software validation report chapter 8.3 and pass release, anomaly, version, environment, completion, archive, and reliable delivery requirements.

evidence.claim: Software risk management was linked to ISO 14971 and included software hazard analysis, causes, risk controls, verification, and traceability.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 7.1 through 7.3 reference Software validation report chapter 11 and pass requirements for identifying software items contributing to hazardous situations in ISO 14971 risk analysis, documenting causes, defining risk controls, verifying risk controls, and traceability.

evidence.claim: The IEC 62304 evidence mapping identifies software validation report A-TCF-5-131 revision 0 dated 2023-11-07 as the main manufacturer document.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment Software mapping lists multiple deliverables with title Software validation report (A-TCF-5-131), revision 0, date 2023-11-07.

evidence.claim: Usability was evaluated under IEC 60601-1-6 with IEC 62366-1 coverage.
evidence.evidenceText: The usability engineering process has been evaluated according to IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + AMD1:2013 + AMD2:2020; all applicable clauses of IEC 62366-1:2015 + AMD1:2020 have been addressed.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1-6 report REP028549_1 was issued by Nemko Korea on 2024-02-20 for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Report REP028549_1 lists date of issue 2024-02-20, testing laboratory Nemko Korea Co., Ltd., test item description Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, and model/type reference A1717MCW; A1417MCW; F1417MCW.

evidence.claim: The IEC 60601-1-6 report used CB Scheme procedure and states the product fulfills IEC 60601-1-6 and EN 60601-1-6 requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of testing states test procedure CB Scheme and product fulfills IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + AMD1:2013 + AMD2:2020 and EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015 + A2:2021.

evidence.claim: The IEC 60601-1-6 report states it is a process standard only with no testing.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of testing lists Tests performed: Process standard only, no testing.

evidence.claim: The usability engineering process included use specification, user-interface safety risk analysis, hazard-related use scenarios, user-interface specification, evaluation plan, implementation, and summative evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: Annex I clauses 5.1 through 5.9 pass use specification, safety-related interface characteristics, hazards and hazardous situations, hazard-related use scenarios, summative evaluation selection, user-interface specification, evaluation plan, implementation, and summative evaluation.

evidence.claim: The user manual is A-TCF-5-130 Version 0.1 dated 15/09/2023 and is provided in English/Korean.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 4 User manual title page states User manual for A1717MCW A1417MCW F1417MCW, A-TCF-5-130 Version: 0.1 15/09/2023, Language: ENGLISH / KOREAN.

evidence.claim: The user manual includes technical description, instructions for use, markings, safety notices, maintenance, cleaning/disinfection, disposal, and version identification information.
evidence.evidenceText: Accompanying documents include instructions for use and technical description; required IFU items, cleaning, maintenance, disposal, and unique version identifier are described in the user manual.

evidence.claim: The detectors include cybersecurity controls for wireless data and access.
evidence.evidenceText: Security section states A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW protects transmitted data using WPA/WPAPSK/WPA2/WPA2PSK variants and restricts random access by setting ID/PW for wireless network connection.

evidence.claim: The detectors are IP56 water-resistant and dustproof according to IEC 60529 when battery cover and wireless mode conditions are met.
evidence.evidenceText: Caution section states the models are rated IP56 according to IEC 60529, and IP56 is guaranteed when the battery cover is closed tightly while wired cable is disconnected and wireless mode is connected.

evidence.claim: The detector passed ingress protection testing as IP54 with subsequent leakage current and dielectric strength checks referenced.
evidence.evidenceText: Classified as IP54 for detector, subjected to IEC 60529 tests; met dielectric strength and leakage current tests with no bridging causing loss of basic safety or essential performance.

evidence.claim: The detector system is Class I or internally powered and has maximum 48 W power consumption.
evidence.evidenceText: Device specification lists rated voltage 100-240 V~, rated frequency 50/60 Hz, electric power consumption MAX 48W, and protection type Class I or internally powered.

evidence.claim: The detector supports wired and wireless digital flat panel operation with auto exposure detection.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Features state the models are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors and support auto exposure detection technology allowing use without generator integration.

evidence.claim: Detector wireless communication supports IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n/ac at 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz.
evidence.evidenceText: Electrical Features list Wi-Fi IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n/ac, 2.4GHz/5GHz.

evidence.claim: The wireless interface supports IEEE 802.11 a/b/g/n/ac, 2.4 GHz/5 GHz dual band, and Open/WEP/WPA/WPA2 PSK/WPA/WPA2 Enterprise security.
evidence.evidenceText: Wireless table lists IEEE 802.11 a/b/g/n/ac Wi-Fi, frequency 2.4GHz/5GHz dual, and security Open, WEP, WPA/WPA2 PSK, WPA/WPA2 Enterprise.

evidence.claim: The detector Wi-Fi 5 GHz function has an EU outdoor-use restriction for 5150-5350 MHz.
evidence.evidenceText: Restriction of use of Wi-Fi using 5 GHz bands (5150 - 5350 MHz): In the EU, the Wi-Fi function in this equipment should not be used outdoors.

evidence.claim: Detector battery performance is specified as 3 hours with a fully charged battery for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Battery section lists battery performance: A1717MCW 3 hours, A1417MCW 3 hours, F1417MCW 3 hours with full charged battery.

evidence.claim: The manufacturer declared compliance with IEC 60601-1-2 for electromagnetic disturbances.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 17 states manufacturer declared equipment compliance with IEC 60601-1-2, Electromagnetic disturbances; compliance documented by manufacturer; verdict N/E.

evidence.claim: The equipment is declared compliant with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards for medical devices in the user manual.
evidence.evidenceText: Compliance statement: This equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards for medical devices.

evidence.claim: CISPR 11 emissions classification is Group 1, Class A.
evidence.evidenceText: Emission table lists RF emissions CISPR 11 Group 1 and RF emissions CISPR 11 Class A for the Wireless Detector.

evidence.claim: Biocompatibility was not evaluated as part of this test report.
evidence.evidenceText: The manufacturer has declared compliance with ISO 10993-1. Biocompatibility is not evaluated as part of this test report; clause 11.7 verdict N/E.

evidence.claim: The equipment is not intended to produce X-radiation, despite being used with X-ray systems for imaging.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 10.1 states Not intended to produce X-radiation; clause 12.4.5.1 states No diagnostic or therapeutic radiation produced.

evidence.claim: The equipment is not intended for oxygen-rich environments, flammable anesthetics, or flammable agents.
evidence.evidenceText: The equipment is not intended for use in oxygen rich environments; not intended for use with flammable anesthetics; not intended for use in conjunction with flammable agents.

evidence.claim: The equipment is not intended to be sterilized by the operator.
evidence.evidenceText: Methods of sterilization: The equipment is not intended to be sterilized by operator; clause 11.6.7 states Not intended to be sterilized.

evidence.claim: Essential performance functions were defined with no unacceptable risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Essential performance table lists image acquisition as intended, no adjacent two-line or blank image, normal operation when required, and manufacturer program operation; remarks: No unacceptable risks.

evidence.claim: Measured input current did not exceed 110% of ratings for listed power configurations.
evidence.evidenceText: Power input tables for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW state measured input current did not exceed 110% of unit ratings.

evidence.claim: Markings were legible and durable after water, ethanol, and isopropyl alcohol rub tests.
evidence.evidenceText: Legibility table states all markings clearly legible from operator position at 500 lx; durability table reports no curling/loosening and remained legible after distilled water, ethanol 96%, and isopropyl alcohol for 15 s.

evidence.claim: Leakage current measurements passed under normal and single fault conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: Leakage current tables list earth leakage, touch current, and patient leakage measurements at 264 V 60 Hz with Pass remarks against NC and SFC limits.

evidence.claim: Dielectric strength testing showed no breakdown for listed MOOP/MOPP insulation areas.
evidence.evidenceText: Dielectric strength table lists areas A-N with 1MOOP, 2MOOP, or 2MOPP, test voltages up to 4000 Vrms, and dielectric breakdown after 1 minute: No.

evidence.claim: Critical components included certified power cords, enclosure materials, power supplies, adapter, battery, switch box, exposure switch, and PCB materials.
evidence.evidenceText: Critical components table lists standards and conformity marks for Longwell and I-SHENG cord sets, DEPS9601 power supply box, BM090S15F AC/DC adapter, ELT-3S1PA battery pack, switch box, exposure switch, and PCB.

evidence.claim: Mechanical strength tests passed for push, impact, drop, and mould stress relief.
evidence.evidenceText: Mechanical strength table reports Pass for 250 N push, 500 g steel ball impact from 1.3 m, hand-held 1 m drop, portable 5 cm drop, and 70 °C 7 h mould stress relief.

evidence.claim: Ingress of water and cleaning tests passed without wetting electrical insulation.
evidence.evidenceText: Ingress/cleaning table lists IP54 detector and cleaning by soft cloth with 70% isopropyl alcohol; no sign of wetting on electrical insulation; post-test dielectric and leakage compliance.

evidence.claim: Single fault short-circuit tests did not create hazardous situations.
evidence.evidenceText: Single fault table states EC455 short-circuit at 264 V for 10 min and rechargeable Li-ion battery output short-circuit at 11.55 Vdc for 10 min caused shutdown, no component damage, no fire/explosion, hazardous situation: No.

evidence.claim: Detector cleaning requires power-off, battery removal, and 70% IPA applied by cloth rather than direct spraying.
evidence.evidenceText: Clean the detector with 70% IPA; before cleaning, turn off the power and separate the battery; do not pour or spray IPA directly on the detector.

evidence.claim: Routine inspection includes daily visual/electrical checks and yearly recalibration.
evidence.evidenceText: Inspection list assigns daily checks for cables, plugs/connectors, cover or part damage, and LED indicator; re-calibration cycle is yearly.

evidence.claim: CB certificate NO82590/A1/M2 covers DEPS9601 as a power supply conforming to IEC 60601-1:2005 and IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD1:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: Certificate NO82590/A1/M2 lists product Power supply, model DEPS9601, and states a sample was tested and found in conformity with IEC 60601-1:2005 and IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD1:2012, test report 352278.

evidence.claim: CB certificate NO96755/M1 covers DEPS9601A as a power supply conforming to IEC 60601-1:2005 and IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD1:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: Certificate NO96755/M1 lists product Power supply, model DEPS9601A, ratings including 100-240 V~ or 24 Vdc input, and conformity with IEC 60601-1:2005 and AMD1:2012, test report 352278.

evidence.claim: Report 352278 is an IEC 60601-1 CB Scheme test report issued by Nemko Korea for DEPS9601 and DEPS9601A.
evidence.evidenceText: Report Number 352278, date of issue 2014-09-18, Amendment No. 2: 2018-04-23; testing laboratory Nemko Korea; test item Power supply; model/type DEPS9601; DEPS9601A; test procedure CB Scheme.

evidence.claim: DEPS9601 is rated 1.0 A, 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz, Class I input and 3 A, 16 V output.
evidence.evidenceText: Ratings for model DEPS9601: Input 1.0 A, 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz, Cl. I; Output 3 A, 16 V.

evidence.claim: DEPS9601A has AC input or 24 Vdc input and outputs 3 A at 16 V.
evidence.evidenceText: Ratings for model DEPS9601A: Input 1.0 A, 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz, Cl. I or 3 A, 24 V; Output 3 A, 16 V.

evidence.claim: DEPS9601A AC input has priority over DC input when both are connected.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of testing states DEPS9601A has AC and 24 Vdc inputs and does not supply AC and DC at the same time; AC source is prior to DC power supply.

evidence.claim: For DEPS9601A, 24 Vdc input source must come from Class I ME equipment complying with IEC 60601-1 in the end product.
evidence.evidenceText: End-product considerations state the DC power source has to be from the Class I ME equipment complied with IEC 60601-1 in the end product.

evidence.claim: The power supply output circuits were not evaluated for direct patient connection.
evidence.evidenceText: Insulation table supplementary note states compliance of SIP/SOP has to be evaluated for end product application and output circuits have not been evaluated for direct patient connection.

evidence.claim: Main report 269183 states the power supply risk management process was not evaluated and must be handled in the end product.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary states risk management files according to ISO 14971:2007 were not provided and not evaluated; risk management shall be considered when equipped as part of the end product.

evidence.claim: The component-level power supply report found no MOPP and considered MOOP for mains-to-secondary operator protection.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.5.1.1 says operator protection (MOOP) provided from mains to secondary; Clause 8.5.1.2 says 'No MOPP'; Clause 8.5.1.3 says 'MOOP considered.'

evidence.claim: The component-level power supply report states no F-type or Type B applied parts were present in the evaluated equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.5.2.1 states 'No Type F-type applied part' and Clause 8.5.2.2 states 'No Type B applied part.'

evidence.claim: Protective earth impedance complied with the 100 mΩ requirement.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 8.6.4 measured 0.019 mΩ from ground pin to potential equalization connector and 0.012 mΩ from ground pin to metal enclosure, against a 100 mΩ allowable limit.

evidence.claim: DEPS9601 passed IEC 60601-1 clause 11.1.1 excessive temperature testing at 90 V / 60 Hz and 264 V / 50 Hz.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 127 and Page 128: '11.1.1 TABLE: Excessive temperatures in ME EQUIPMENT (continued) P'; Model No. DEPS9601; test supply voltage/frequency 90 V / 60 Hz and 264 V / 50 Hz.

evidence.claim: Electrical single fault condition testing for +16 Vdc output shorted to accessible metal enclosure produced no hazard.
evidence.evidenceText: Page 131: Short circuit '+16 Vdc output to accessible metal enclosure', input 264 V, 10 min; 'All output shut downed immediately. No hazard. NO'.

evidence.claim: Attachment 6 includes IECEE CB certificate DK-51428-A1-M1-UL for a BridgePower AC/DC adaptor with rated input 100-240 Vac, 50-60 Hz, 1.3 A.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 6 certificate lists Ref. Certif. No. DK-51428-A1-M1-UL, product AC/DC Adaptor, applicant BRIDGEPOWER CORP, rated input 100-240 Vac, 50-60Hz, 1.3A.

evidence.claim: IECEE CB certificate DK-61570-M1-UL covers a BridgePower AC/DC adaptor under IEC 60601-1 and IEC 60601-1-6.
evidence.evidenceText: Certificate DK-61570-M1-UL states the sample conformed to IEC 60601-1(ed.3), IEC 60601-1(ed.3);am1, IEC 60601-1-6(ed.3), and IEC 60601-1-6(ed.3);am1.

evidence.claim: The BridgePower AC/DC adaptor provides 2MOPP isolation from primary to secondary/enclosure and 1MOPP from primary to earth for Class I construction.
evidence.evidenceText: General product information states the AC-DC adaptor provides '2MOPP isolation from Primary to Secondary/Enclosure' and '1MOPP isolation from Primary to Earth (for Class I construction)'.

evidence.claim: The BridgePower AC/DC adaptor was considered IP22 for harmful ingress of water.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 6 technical considerations state 'The degree of protection against harmful ingress of water is: IP22.'

evidence.claim: Rechargeable Li-ion battery pack ELT-3S1PA was CB certified to IEC 62133:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: CB Test Certificate DK-70440-M1-UL states model/type 3ICP5/43/97, ELT-3S1PA, ratings 11.55 Vdc, 3200 mAh, 36.96 Wh; sample tested and found in conformity with IEC 62133:2012.

evidence.claim: The CB certificate DK-70440-UL covers a rechargeable Li-ion battery pack model ELT-3S1PA / 3ICP5/43/97.
evidence.evidenceText: Product: Rechargeable Li-ion Battery Pack; Model / Type Ref.: ELT-3S1PA, 3ICP5/43/97.

evidence.claim: The battery pack is rated 11.55 Vdc, 3200 mAh, and 36.96 Wh.
evidence.evidenceText: Ratings and principal characteristics: 11.55 Vdc, 3200 mAh, 36.96 Wh.

evidence.claim: The IEC 62133 test report was issued on 2018-02-01 and contains 26 pages.
evidence.evidenceText: Report Number: HCT-BA-1801-CB002; Date of issue: 2018-02-01; Total number of pages: 26 pages.

evidence.claim: The product fulfils IEC 62133:2012 and EN 62133:2013.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of compliance states: The product fulfils the requirements of IEC 62133:2012 and EN 62133:2013.

evidence.claim: The battery design uses a 3S-1P rechargeable Li-ion cell assembly and the cell model PGF494397H++ was tested to IEC 62133:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: Electronic components mounted on PWB with a 3S-1P Rechargeable Li-ion cell; Battery cell Model: PGF494397H++ is tested according to IEC 62133:2012.

evidence.claim: External short circuit, free fall, and over-charging battery tests passed with no fire or explosion.
evidence.evidenceText: 8.3.2 External short circuit results: No fire. No explosion; 8.3.3 Free fall results: No fire. No explosion; 8.3.6 Over-charging of battery results: No fire. No explosion; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Transport compliance documentation for UN Recommendations on Transport of Dangerous Goods was provided.
evidence.evidenceText: 8.3.8 Transport tests: Manufacturer’s documentation provided to show compliance with UN Recommendations on Transport of Dangerous Goods; See the enclosure Test report for transport; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Battery marking requirements passed, including marking designation 3ICP5/43/97 and caution statement in the user manual.
evidence.evidenceText: 10.2 Battery marking: Batteries marked in accordance with requirements; 3ICP5/43/97; Batteries marked with an appropriate caution statement; Refer to the user manual; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Additional attachment pages identify detector input-source block and circuit diagrams under report number REP028549.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 8 Block diagram and circuit diagram, pages 1-3 of 3, Report No. REP028549, include block diagram and circuit diagram related input source on the detector.

reviewFlags.reason: Biocompatibility is manufacturer-declared and marked N/E in this IEC 60601-1 report; do not treat it as independently evaluated.

EMC compliance to IEC 60601-1-2 is manufacturer-declared/documented by manufacturer in the IEC 60601-1 report; detailed EMC results are not included in the extracted evidence, although the user manual includes EMC guidance.

Power supply component reports defer direct patient connection, MOPP, SIP/SOP, leakage current in end product, installation suitability, biocompatibility, EMC, and risk management end-use compliance to the final medical electrical equipment evaluation.

Several source statements identify the product as a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector while some national-deviation rows state 'Not an X-Ray system' for X-ray system-specific clauses; preserve this distinction when using the evidence.
The IEC 60601-1-6 report states 'process standard only, no testing'; usability compliance is based on process/file inspection rather than physical testing.

The source contains the main detector CB certificate/report plus multiple component attachments for power supplies, AC/DC adapter, and battery pack; component-level claims should be distinguished from detector-level claims.
Korea national differences are attached for information or listed in later attachments; national-deviation scope differs by report and component attachment.
Some battery and transformer attachment text appears OCR-corrupted, but key certificate fields, models, ratings, report numbers, dates, and standards are legible in the extracted evidence.
